thesis_card.headline: NVDA remains structurally attractive despite near-term China export control risks
thesis_card.why_now: While U.S. export controls under consideration present a material near-term headwind to China data-center sales and pose inventory/guidance risks, NVDA's dominant AI accelerator position and TAM expansion in enterprise/cloud computing provide fundamental support. The 72% confidence reflects conviction in long-term thesis but acknowledgment of geopolitical uncertainty.
thesis_card.expected_edge: NVDA's architectural lead in high-performance computing and embedded AI advantage should sustain premium valuations post-policy clarity. If export controls are less restrictive than feared, upside re-rating; if implemented broadly, China revenue headwind is already partially priced in given recent volatility.
thesis_card.risks: 1) U.S. export controls could force material China revenue write-downs and FY guidance cuts, creating 15%+ stock volatility; 2) Inventory corrections at customers if sales forecasts revised downward; 3) Competitor gains (AMD, custom silicon) if supply disruptions extend; 4) Macro AI spending slowdown coupled with geopolitical pressure creates compounding downside.

key_evidence.quote: U.S. export controls under consideration could materially impact China sales, which represent a meaningful share of data-center revenue, and may force inventory write-downs and near-term guidance cuts
